channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jim Larrañaga stepping down at Miami, Bill Courtney to take over, AP source says
item.description: CORAL GABLES, Fla. (AP) — Jim Larrañaga is stepping down as Miami’s men’s basketball coach effective immediately, a person with knowledge of the decision said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Erling Haaland misses penalty and Man City drops more points after 1-1 draw with Everton
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Erling Haaland's penalty miss cost Manchester City as the four-time defending Premier League champion dropped more points in a 1-1 draw with Everton on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Every team Detroit Lions could face in their first playoff game
item.description: The Detroit Lions don't know their playoff seed just yet, but either way, there are several teams they could end up playing in their first matchup. Here's the full list and how each can happen.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: Durant and Beal score 27 points each, Suns beat Nuggets 110-100 to close out Christmas slate
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Kevin Durant and Bradley Beal each scored 27 points and the Phoenix Suns beat the Denver Nuggets 110-100 on Wednesday night in the nightcap to the NBA’s Christmas slate of games.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lakers big man Anthony Davis exits Christmas game with sprained left ankle
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — LeBron James and the Lakers delivered once more when big man Anthony Davis went down. Davis didn't return for Los Angeles against Golden State on Wednesday night after spraining his left ankle midway through the first quarter.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LeBron James scores 31 in record 19th Christmas Day game as Lakers hold off Curry, Warriors 115-113
item.description: Austin Reaves scored the winning layup with one second left after Stephen Curry tied it on a 31-foot 3-pointer with seven seconds remaining, LeBron James had 31 points and 10 assists playing in his NBA-record 19th Christmas Day game, and the Los Angeles Lakers held off the Golden State Warriors 115-
item.dc:creator: Janie Mccauley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Comanche, defending champion LawConnect lead early in Sydney to Hobart yacht race
item.description: Comanche has taken an eight nautical-mile lead over defending line honors champion and fellow 100-foot super maxi LawConnect early in the Sydney to Hobart yacht race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texans look for answers after embarrassing 31-2 home loss to the Ravens
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Quarterback C.J. Stroud summed up Houston’s performance in an embarrassing 31-2 loss to Baltimore on Wednesday. “Sometimes you go out there and lay an egg,” Stroud said. “It’s not easy to say that, but that’s what happened. Didn’t play well from the first play to the last.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lamar Jackson breaks NFL QB rushing record, accounts for 3 TDs in Ravens' 31-2 romp over Texans
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Lamar Jackson broke the NFL career rushing record for quarterbacks in the Baltimore Ravens' victory over the Houston Texans on Wednesday. A highlight came when Jackson dashed 48 yards for a touchdown on a run where he reached a career-best top speed of 21.25 mph.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jackson breaks Vick's NFL rushing record for QBs in Ravens' rout over Texans
item.description: Baltimore’s Lamar Jackson passed Michael Vick for the most yards rushing by a quarterback in NFL history in a 31-2 win over the Houston Texans in Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump floats NHL legend Wayne Gretzky as Canadian prime minister amid Trudeau turmoil
item.description: President-elect Trump expressed his support for former NHL great Wayne Gretzky as prime minister of Canada in a post on social media on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bridges scores season-high 41, Knicks withstand Wembanyama's monster Christmas debut to edge Spurs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mikal Bridges scored a season-high 41 points, and the New York Knicks overcame 42 from Victor Wembanyama in a monster Christmas debut to edge the San Antonio Spurs 117-114 on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netflix under pressure with Christmas Day NFL slate after Tyson-Paul streaming debacle
item.description: Netflix is under a lot of pressure for Wednesday's NFL Christmas doubleheader after many subscribers had issues watching the Mike Tyson-Jake Paul fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bears to host Inspire Change Game at final home game of the 2024 season
item.description: The NFL's Inspire Change initiative is hosting a game at Soldier Field to raise awareness for social justice causes, featuring players wearing "Inspire Change" gear, special edition merchandise, and in-stadium activations.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Eight new WNBA coaches now in place. In 2025, 7 women and 6 men will be in charge on the bench
item.description: The WNBA now has all of its head coaching spots filled after there were eight open positions this offseason. Dallas named Chris Koclanes as its head coach on Monday and then an hour later Washington hired Sydney Johnson as its new head, rounding out the seven hirings.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark honored as AP Female Athlete of the Year following her impact on women's sports
item.description: Caitlin Clark raised the profile of women's basketball to unprecedented levels in both the college ranks and the WNBA, and Tuesday she was named the AP Female Athlete of the Year for her impact on and off the court.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Houston Texans wide receiver Tank Dell released from hospital after severe knee injury
item.description: It’s the second straight year Tank Dell has sustained what is almost certain to be a season-ending injury.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Flames get goals from 6 players in a 6-4 victory over the Blackhawks
item.description: Jonathan Huberdeau, Connor Zary, Blake Coleman, and Matt Coronato each had a goal and an assist as the Calgary Flames beat the Chicago Blackhawks 6-4 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bulls face the Celtics on 3-game win streak
item.description: Boston Celtics (21-6, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (13-15, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: Chicago will look to keep its three-game win streak going when the Bulls take on Boston.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utes land talented running back Wayshawn Parker
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes football team may have picked up its biggest off-season acquisition yet. Washington State running back Wayshawn Parker, considered one of the top backs in the transfer portal, announced he will play for the Utes next season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Brock Purdy gets his offensive line new cars for Christmas
item.description: (KRON) -- It's the most wonderful time of the year -- especially if you're a San Francisco 49ers offensive lineman. Quarterback Brock Purdy hooked the big dawgs up with brand-new Toyota cars, a video posted by the 49ers on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Bay FC Treasure Island training facility lease approved by SF mayor
item.description: (BCN) --San Francisco Mayor London Breed signed legislation on Thursday that will allow the women's professional soccer team Bay Football Club, or Bay FC, to make Treasure Island their future home with a permanent training center.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Cody Bellinger experienced World Series title with dad Clay at old Yankee Stadium
item.description: Bellinger won the 2019 NL MVP award with the Los Angeles Dodgers after hitting .305 with 47 homers, 115 RBIs and a 1.035 OPS.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9
